@graph.rdfs:label: Ironhold
schema:description: A brutal industrial hive city built around massive underground ore deposits and surface-level refineries. Ironhold is perpetual motion—furnaces roar, pneumatic lifts scream, and cargo shuttles arc overhead 24/7. The city is a machine for converting raw materials into processed goods and shipping them off-world.
Population: ~380 million workers, engineers, and support staff. Majority are dwarves, humans, and goblinoids adapted to industrial labor. Low social strata; high turnover due to workplace hazards.

Geography: Lower city is mining and processing (geothermal-powered, brutally hot). Mid-city is worker housing and supply distribution (utilitarian, crowded). Upper city is administrative and freight hubs (extensive cargo aerodock for off-world shipping).

Economy: Ore extraction and refinement, industrial fabrication, freight logistics. Exports processed metals, refined components, and bulk goods. Imports luxury goods (rarely), replacement machinery, and migrant labor.

Culture: Pragmatic, hierarchical, union-dominated. Status is measured by skill, safety record, and tenure. Work defines identity; leisure is brief and often involves gambling or black-market substances.

Dominant Power: Forge Syndicate (labor union with absolute veto over production). Other presences: Armanitech (procurement office and equipment sales), Brug's Gobbos (shadows on every dock, black-market supplies), independent salvagers.

sa:containsLocation.rdfs:label: The Thermite Pits
sa:containsLocation.schema:description: Underground mining and open-pit excavations extracting ore directly. Geothermal vents power the deepest refineries. Dangerous, relentless work.

sa:containsLocation.rdfs:label: The Forge District
sa:containsLocation.schema:description: Massive industrial furnaces and smelting operations. The air reeks of metal and sulfur. This is the economic heart.

sa:containsLocation.rdfs:label: Worker's Haven
sa:containsLocation.schema:description: Dense lower residential district. Cheap, utilitarian housing blocks stacked vertically. Syndicate union halls and communal dining centers.

sa:containsLocation.rdfs:label: The Cargo Towers
sa:containsLocation.schema:description: Massive freight elevator shafts and aerodock facilities connecting to orbital freighters. Where processed goods are loaded for off-world shipment.

sa:containsLocation.rdfs:label: The Undercity
sa:containsLocation.schema:description: Black-market district hidden within abandoned mining shafts and tunnel networks. Where gray-market goods change hands and Syndicate discipline doesn't reach.
